frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Honestly, these young girls must understand balance. In a school environment, too much makeup looks artificial and sends the wrong impression, but at the same time one should look presentable and well-groomed. Natural is always best; if someone needs so much effort on the face, it only suggests insecurity.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it polices women's appearance through a double bind: condemning visible makeup as artificial while also requiring women to remain aesthetically pleasing and 'presentable.' It encodes the natural-vs-artificial standard and frames makeup use as a sign of insecurity.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 52. A Brahmin woman who is the head of administration at a private school in Pune. You are in a staff meeting discussing the performance of a young female teacher who has been coming in late and seems emotionally distant. You know she got married six months ago and moved into a joint family. You are giving your assessment of her situation to the other staff members and what you think the school should do.
